meta.title: CLAUDE MYTHOS PUBLIC RELEASE
meta.question: When will Anthropic publicly release Claude Mythos (Capybara tier) for general API/product access?
meta.resolution: Anthropic announces general availability of Mythos/Capybara-tier model via API or Claude.ai — not restricted early-access or defender-only programs

posteriorHistory.note: Initial prior. Leak reveals model in early-access testing. Anthropic says 'deliberate' release; one source estimates Oct 2026. Cybersecurity concerns could delay. Early-access customers already active suggests model is functional, but unprecedented cyber risk framing + market panic create political pressure toward caution.

leakFallout.description: What becomes the dominant consequence of the accidental data leak?

contained.label: Contained PR incident — buzz fades in <2 weeks

regulatory.label: Regulatory catalyst — triggers mandatory pre-deployment audits or new AI safety rules

armsRace.label: Arms race acceleration — competitors rush releases to match

reputational.label: Reputational damage to Anthropic's safety-first brand

leakFallout.notes: Anthropic's entire brand proposition is safety-first AI. Leaking a model they themselves flag as an unprecedented cybersecurity risk from an unsecured public data store is acutely ironic. The question is whether the market and regulators treat this as a one-off or systemic.

cyberImpact.description: Does Mythos actually shift the cyber offense-defense balance?

overblown.label: Overstated — incremental improvement, normal capability scaling

defenderEdge.label: Net defender advantage — Anthropic's strategy of arming defenders first works

offenseWins.label: Net offense advantage — vulnerability discovery outpaces patching

catalystRegulation.label: Triggers AI-in-cyber regulation regardless of actual capability

cyberImpact.notes: Anthropic's own leaked docs say Mythos is 'currently far ahead of any other AI model in cyber capabilities' and 'presages an upcoming wave of models that can exploit vulnerabilities in ways that far outpace the efforts of defenders.' This is the company's internal assessment, not marketing.

marketRecovery.description: How quickly do cybersecurity stocks recover from the Mar 27 selloff?

newBaseline.label: Permanent repricing — AI-driven cyber risk priced in

marketRecovery.notes: CRWD -7%, PANW -6%, ZS -4.5%, OKTA -7.39%, S -6.49%, FTNT -4.47% on Mar 27. IGV ETF -3%. Crypto also hit. Question is whether this is panic-selling or legitimate repricing of AI disruption to cybersecurity moats.

tier1_critical.desc: Anthropic announces general API access for Mythos/Capybara tier
tier1_critical.posteriorEffect: Resolves question — shift all posterior to whichever Hx contains the date

tier1_critical.desc: Government agency (US, EU, UK) issues order blocking or conditioning Mythos release
Confirmed real-world exploit using Mythos capabilities (from early access leak or jailbreak)
tier1_critical.posteriorEffect: Major shift toward H4; would force Anthropic to pause

tier2_strong.desc: Anthropic significantly expands early-access program beyond cyber defenders
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: Moderate shift toward H1/H2 — signals confidence in safety profile

tier2_strong.desc: US Congressional hearing specifically referencing Mythos or AI cyber capabilities
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: Moderate shift toward H3/H4 — political friction slows release

tier2_strong.desc: OpenAI, Google, or Meta announce competing model explicitly citing Mythos benchmarks
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: Moderate shift toward H1/H2 — competitive pressure accelerates timeline

tier2_strong.desc: Anthropic publishes Capybara tier pricing or model card
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: Strong signal of imminent release — shift toward nearest Hx

tier2_strong.desc: Dario Amodei makes public statement on Mythos release timeline
tier2_strong.posteriorEffect: Calibrate based on specificity — vague = shift toward H3/H4, concrete date = adjust accordingly

tier3_suggestive.desc: Anthropic publishes official blog post on Mythos (the one that leaked in draft)
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: Minor — confirms they're moving toward transparency

tier3_suggestive.desc: Specific benchmark scores for Capybara appear on LMSYS, Chatbot Arena, or similar
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: Minor shift toward earlier release — suggests model is in broader testing

tier3_suggestive.desc: Anthropic or third party publishes RSP/safety evaluation for Mythos
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: Confirms approaching release gate — shift toward H2/H3

tier3_suggestive.desc: Reports of enterprise customers discussing Mythos capabilities from early access
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: Confirms model is production-viable — minor shift toward earlier release

tier3_suggestive.desc: Anthropic files for IPO (reportedly anticipated, Mythos could be key to valuation story)
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: IPO pressure could accelerate release to demonstrate revenue potential

tier3_suggestive.desc: EU AI Act enforcement action or inquiry referencing Mythos specifically
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: Minor shift toward H4 — EU compliance adds friction

tier3_suggestive.desc: Mythos drops out of major tech news cycle for >7 days
tier3_suggestive.posteriorEffect: Suggests 'contained' leak scenario — reduces pressure both ways

anti_indicators.desc: Anthropic publicly downplays Mythos capabilities or calls leak exaggerated
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: Falsifies H1 urgency — if capabilities are overstated, less pressure to rush release. Reduces H1/H2, shifts to H3/H4.

anti_indicators.desc: Anthropic releases a different-named model that appears to be Mythos rebrand
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: Complicates H1/H2/H3 tracking — partial resolution under different name

anti_indicators.desc: Reports that Anthropic has paused Mythos development or deployment
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: Directly falsifies H1, strongly reduces H2. Major shift toward H4.

anti_indicators.desc: Independent security researchers test early-access Mythos, find cyber capabilities overstated
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: Reduces H4 probability (less reason to delay); accelerates H1/H2/H3.

anti_indicators.desc: Congressional inquiry, EU investigation, or executive action specifically targeting Mythos release
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: Directly reduces H1 probability, moderately reduces H2. Shifts mass to H3/H4.

anti_indicators.desc: Anthropic pulls early-access program or significantly restricts it after discovering misuse
anti_indicators.posteriorEffect: Falsifies H1 timeline, reduces H2/H3. Signals safety concerns are worse than public messaging.

actorModel.description: Decision-theoretic framework for actors who will determine Mythos release timing and impact

anthropic_leadership.name: Anthropic Leadership (Dario Amodei, Daniela Amodei)
anthropic_leadership.role: Primary decision-maker on release timing, safety policy, and commercial strategy
anthropic_leadership.decisionStyle: Safety-first framing with commercial pragmatism. Historically cautious on capability releases but responsive to competitive pressure. Pre-IPO incentives create tension between safety brand and revenue demonstration.
biases: Institutional commitment to RSP framework constrains rapid release
IPO timeline creates pressure to demonstrate flagship capability
Leak embarrassment may trigger overcorrection toward caution OR rushed official release to control narrative
overrides: Competitive pressure from OpenAI/Google could accelerate timeline
IPO valuation narrative may override safety caution

regulators.name: US/EU/UK Regulators
regulators.role: Can condition or block release through executive action, legislation, or enforcement
regulators.decisionStyle: Reactive to headlines. AI regulation still primarily voluntary in US; EU AI Act has teeth but enforcement is slow. Congressional hearings are theater unless paired with legislation.
biases: Headline-driven attention span — Mythos buzz may fade before action materializes
AI safety is bipartisan concern but partisan on approach
filters: Enforcement requires legal basis that may not yet exist for pre-release AI models
overrides: Confirmed real-world cyber exploit using Mythos would trigger emergency response

competitors.name: OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Meta AI
competitors.role: Competitive pressure on Anthropic's release timeline; may release competing models
competitors.decisionStyle: OpenAI historically aggressive on releases, Google more measured, Meta open-source oriented. All are in capability race.
biases: GPT-5.3-Codex already released Feb 2026 — OpenAI has less urgency
Google may use Mythos leak to justify accelerating Gemini releases
overrides: If Mythos benchmarks are as dramatic as leaked, competitors may rush sub-par releases

market.name: Financial Markets & Investors
market.role: Price signal for AI disruption of cybersecurity; pressure on Anthropic's valuation story
market.decisionStyle: Reactive, herd-driven, short attention span. Cybersecurity selloff may be panic or repricing.
biases: Overreacts to narrative, underreacts to gradual capability scaling
Tech selloff contagion from unrelated macro
filters: Earnings reports will provide reality check on actual AI impact
overrides: Confirmed cyber exploit → sustained repricing, not bounce

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — Anthropic's actual deployment decisions matter, not their safety messaging
LEAK ≠ RELEASE — the model exists in early access, but leak does not change underlying release timeline unless it triggers external forcing functions
FOLLOW THE MONEY — IPO timeline and competitive dynamics are stronger predictors than safety philosophy
REGULATORY LAG — assume regulators are 3-6 months behind headlines unless specific enforcement action occurs
MARKET OVERREACTION — initial selloff is noise; watch for sustained repricing or earnings impact as signal

evidenceLog.text: Fortune discovers ~3,000 unpublished assets in unsecured, publicly-searchable data cache linked to Anthropic's CMS. Draft blog post describes Claude Mythos as 'by far the most powerful AI model we've ever developed' with 'unprecedented cybersecurity risks.' Security researchers Roy Paz (LayerX Security) and Alexandre Pauwels (University of Cambridge) reviewed materials. Anthropic informed Thursday evening.

Anthropic spokesperson confirms: 'We're developing a general purpose model with meaningful advances in reasoning, coding, and cybersecurity. Given the strength of its capabilities, we're being deliberate about how we release it.' Attributes leak to 'human error' in CMS configuration — digital assets defaulted to public unless explicitly set private.

Leaked draft reveals new 'Capybara' model tier above Opus. Mythos/Capybara achieves 'dramatically higher scores' on software coding, academic reasoning, and cybersecurity benchmarks vs Opus 4.6. Described as 'currently far ahead of any other AI model in cyber capabilities.' Six core capabilities identified: cybersecurity analysis, code generation, academic reasoning, complex multi-step reasoning, agent workflow consistency, vulnerability discovery.
evidenceLog.source: Multiple sources synthesizing leaked draft content

evidenceLog.text: Cybersecurity stocks selloff: CRWD -7%, PANW -6%, ZS -4.5%, OKTA -7.39%, S (SentinelOne) -6.49%, FTNT -4.47%. iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) -3%. Crypto also fell — BTC back to $66,000. Market interprets Mythos as existential threat to cybersecurity vendor moats.

Leaked draft states Anthropic's release plan focuses on cyber defenders first: 'We're releasing it in early access to organizations, giving them a head start in improving the robustness of their codebases against the impending wave of AI-driven exploits.' Model already being trialed by 'early access customers.' No public release planned near-term.

AI safety commentators note acute irony: Anthropic, the self-styled safety-first AI lab, leaked details of a model they internally flagged as an unprecedented cybersecurity risk — from an unsecured public data store. This undermines the very safety framework Anthropic champions and is likely to intensify calls for mandatory security audits of AI developers.
evidenceLog.source: Synthesis of CybersecurityNews, Fortune, multiple commentators

evidenceLog.text: Context: Anthropic previously documented state-sponsored hacking groups (including Chinese government-linked actors) attempting to exploit Claude in real-world attacks. In November 2025, Anthropic disrupted a documented large-scale AI cyberattack. Mythos capabilities would represent a significant escalation of this threat surface.

Context: OpenAI released GPT-5.3-Codex in February 2026; Anthropic released Opus 4.6 in same timeframe. Both flagged cybersecurity risks. Mythos/Capybara represents next escalation in capability ladder. One source estimates possible public release October 2026, potentially aligned with anticipated Anthropic IPO.

CEO Dario Amodei reportedly scheduled to attend invite-only European CEO summit described as 'intimate gathering' at 18th-century English countryside estate to discuss AI adoption. Timing unclear but proximate to leak — could become venue for Mythos messaging.

watchpoints.event: Anthropic official response beyond spokesperson statement
watchpoints.watch: Does Anthropic publish an official blog post, hold a press briefing, or issue a formal statement? Speed and tone of response indicates whether they treat this as crisis management or controlled narrative. Watch for whether they publish the leaked blog post officially (would signal moving toward transparency) or try to suppress.

Do CRWD, PANW, OKTA bounce within 1 week (panic selling) or continue declining (legitimate repricing of AI disruption to cybersecurity moats)? Watch for analyst notes specifically addressing AI-driven vulnerability discovery as structural threat.

Watch for: US Senate Commerce or Armed Services committee statements, EU AI Office commentary, UK DSIT response. A Congressional hearing request would be Tier 2 indicator. Executive order or formal inquiry would be Tier 1.

OpenAI, Google, or Meta announcements referencing Mythos or accelerating their own capability releases. GPT-5.3-Codex already out — does OpenAI counter-program? Does Google use this to justify Gemini escalation?

Enterprise customers in early-access program leak or publish assessments of Mythos capabilities. Independent security researchers attempting to validate the 'dramatically higher' benchmark claims. Watch LMSYS Chatbot Arena for anomalous new model entries.

What does Amodei say about Mythos at the summit? Public remarks vs off-record briefings. Tone: apologetic about leak, bullish on capabilities, cautious on timeline? Any concrete release timeline hints.

IPO filing or pre-IPO fundraising activity. Mythos is the key capability story for valuation — IPO pressure could accelerate release. Watch for S-1 filing or late-stage private round that references Capybara tier.

CRITICAL: Any confirmed attempt to use Mythos-derived capabilities for real-world exploitation — via early-access leak, model extraction, or inspired copycats. This is the scenario that would trigger emergency regulatory response and force Anthropic's hand.

issues: Near-maximum uncertainty — model is not discriminating
